Principles, understanding these two pillars that sit side by side are what really drive how successful you'll be with getting leads in the door. So let's get to it. So the lead nurture process is a combination of speed and volume. So one of our core tenants at Gym Launch in general still is Speed is King and that's been carried over since my days at the Gym. Speed is King. These are some of the best practices that we know. You want to make sure that you call, you already have your Zepier SMS set up so that your phone should buzz the moment you have a new lead and you should immediately tap to call a double dial immediately upon receiving every lead. Make sure that you always follow FTC guidelines at the time of this making. It's 8am to 9pm in terms of call hour. Speed is how much you work. So if you can stay on it then you will have to call way less and spend less time calling you more time selling. The other thing to lead from the time they opt in to the time they schedule and the time that you have reached out to them, the dramatic increase in likelihood that they will actually show up for the sale. So speed increases the proximity to their pain and increases the proximity to their interest. Less aware of their pain they are, the less interested they are in the thing that they opt in into. So if you opt in and I call you within 5 minutes, you remember exactly what I'm talking about, you can schedule, you can come in that afternoon. If you opt in and I don't really call you till day 2, I mean tomorrow I'd actually go down. But speed is King. Speed is King. You want to work less? Be faster. Second piece is volume is King and the reason I didn't say Queen is because they are twins, they are twin pillars to making this work and making a lot of money. This is going to be your competitive advantage. The reason I have workload realities is the second point here is that this will be the work. This is the work. This is the work that you will succeed or not. I'm just being very candid. The more reach outs you have, the more people, the much money you will make is the number of reach outs you have. So if you want a decrease in the amount of reach outs you have, call faster. But still the number one driver, once we've looked at all these campaigns, is directly proportional with how many calls are being made, how many reach outs are being made. So just a quick, you see the second main set of bullet points is volume, money, math. Ten dollars of a call is, so we've tracked calls over hundreds of campaigns when we were running these on our own and that is the math. So for every, mind you, you can do 50 calls in an hour or you can take you all day. But the question is, would you rather make more money or less? So ten dollars per call, so if you fail, every single time you dial the phone or the same thing for your employee. So if you had set the goal to make $50,000 for a launch, that would mean 100 sales. That would mean you should have 25% of your sales, which is a great conversion number. If you're doing 25%, you're doing awesome. So if you have 100 sales and you sell 500, that's $50,000 a day and following our $10 we'd have 5,000 calls over 30 days, which means your action item is 167 calls a day if you have a one month long campaign. That's what it boils down to. You can forget everything else that's TC7 calls a day, period. So that means you could just call for four hours a day, 40 calls per hour for four hours, roughly what I make. So speed and volume, those are the primary principles of this process. I mean it. Volume and speed.
